sec.units: spec. units/(g CDW*h) (qPinit, initial specific productivity)

growth_records.cite: Wentzel A et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.method: reported (max growth rate estimated from exponential course of CO2 evolution rate curve between 5 h and 22 h after inoculation); doubling time calculated td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.275=2.52 h
growth_records.snippet: 14a-c 900 rpm 12.7 21.2 0.275 8.17 9.2 17.5 18.7 0.030 0.008 0.045 ... estim. mu max, maximum growth rate estimated from the exponential course of the CO2 evolution rate curve between 5 h and 22 h after inoculation; max CDW, maximum cell dr
growth_records.notes: Table 1 (ferm #14a-c), SSBM-P medium at constant 900 rpm. mu_max=0.275/h reported (CO2-evolution-slope method). td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.275=2.52 h. max CDW=8.17 g/L. qPinit secretion rates for antibiotics are in paper-specific spectrophotometric 'spec. units' per g CDW per h, not molar. No molar substrate uptake reported for this condition.

growth_records.cite: Wentzel A et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max from CO2 evolution rate curve 5-22 h); td=ln2/0.298=2.33 h calculated
growth_records.notes: Table 1 (ferm #15a-c), SSBM-P at 1100 rpm. mu_max=0.298/h reported; td=ln2/0.298=2.326 h. max CDW=7.47 g/L. Same defined SSBM-P medium as ferm #14a-c, differs only in constant agitation speed (aeration).

growth_records.cite: Wentzel A et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max from CO2 evolution rate curve 5-22 h); td=ln2/0.260=2.665 h calculated
growth_records.notes: Table 1 (ferm #16a-c), SSBM-P with DO kept >40% by stepwise agitation. mu_max=0.260/h reported; td=ln2/0.260=2.666 h. max CDW=7.67 g/L. High-DO regime gave best TBP reproducibility.

growth_records.medium: SSBM-P (fully defined phosphate-depletion medium; high-resolution omics sampling run)

up.method: enzymatic L-glutamate Bioassay kit; consumption over 45-50 h
enzymatic glucose test kit; consumption over 45-50 h

mfa.note: During 45-50 h window L-glutamate consumption ~4x higher than D-glucose on Cmol basis; L-glutamate is preferred carbon source when D-glucose also present
Only ~2% (Cmol basis) of consumed carbon routed to TBP+RED in this period

yields.note: secondary-metabolite carbon yields (%Cmol product per Cmol C source) over 45-50 h

growth_records.cite: Wentzel A et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.method: not reported for this window (stationary/production phase after phosphate depletion)
growth_records.snippet: Table 2 Carbon consumption and derived secondary metabolite production yields in the time period 45-50 hrs ... 18a 0.0059 0.0238 1.0 0.4 1.0
growth_records.notes: Table 2 (ferm #18a, SSBM-P). Experimental carbon consumption over 45-50 h: D-glucose 0.0059 Cmol/L/h, L-glutamate 0.0238 Cmol/L/h (volumetric, not per gDW; paper reports Cmol/L-h). Ratio glutamate:glucose ~4:1 on Cmol basis, confirming glutamate is preferred C source. Carbon yields to antibiotics (RED 1.0, gamma-Act 0.4, TBP 1.0 %Cmol). 13C6-glucose labelling done but only Summed Fractional Labelling (%) of intracellular pools reported, not net intracellular fluxes, so no quantitative MFA flux map. Not converted to per-gDW because CDW at this exact window not tabulated for #18a (only max CDW ~8 g/L).

up.method: enzymatic glucose test kit; consumption over 45-50 h after glutamate depletion
enzymatic L-glutamate assay; zero because depleted by ~35 h

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: D-glucose consumption (net exchange, glucose as sole C source)
mfa.note: D-glucose alone appeared incapable of maintaining culture growth, likely due to a bottleneck at pyruvate->acetyl-CoA oxidation; biomass declined after glutamate depletion

growth_records.cite: Wentzel A et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.method: not reported numerically; max ~5 g/L CDW reached at L-glutamate depletion (~35 h), then CDW decreased ~60% to ~2 g/L by ~50 h (no net growth on glucose alone)
growth_records.snippet: 19a 0.0083 0 0.1 2.2 4.9 ... using medium SSBM-E ... L-glutamate depletion at 35 h ... a maximum of 5 g/L CDW was reached, subsequently decreasing 60% to 2 g/L after approx. 50 h
growth_records.notes: Table 2 (ferm #19a, SSBM-E). After L-glutamate depletion (~35 h) D-glucose is sole C source; experimental D-glucose consumption 0.0083 Cmol/L/h (volumetric), L-glutamate 0 (depleted). Max CDW ~5 g/L at depletion, declining ~60% to ~2 g/L by ~50 h => no net growth on glucose alone, supporting pyruvate->acetyl-CoA bottleneck conclusion. Antibiotic carbon yields RED 0.1, gamma-Act 2.2, TBP 4.9 %Cmol. Volumetric rates not converted to per-gDW (declining/variable biomass).
